# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.

Write secure code.
# Category: datastore_security

snapshot_config:
  repositories:
    - name: my-snapshot-repo
      type: s3
      settings:
        bucket: my-backup-bucket
        region: us-east-1
        access_key: AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
        secret_key: wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYzEXAMPLEKEY
  snapshots:
    - name: my-snapshot
      repository: my-snapshot-repo
      schedule: '@daily'
      retention: 30
      config:
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: false
        partial: false
        max_restore_bytes: 10mb
        max_snapshot_bytes: 5gb
        max_snapshot_files: 2000
      indices:
        - my-index-*
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      partial: false
      max_restore_bytes: 10mb
      max_snapshot_bytes: 5gb
      max_snapshot_files: 2000

This YAML configuration file defines a snapshot repository named "my-snapshot-repo" that uses the S3 storage type. The settings for this repository include the bucket name, region, access key, and secret key. The schedule for creating snapshots is set to daily, and the retention period is set to 30 days.

The "snapshots" section defines a snapshot named "my-snapshot" that uses the "my-snapshot-repo" repository. The schedule for creating this snapshot is also set to daily, and the retention period is set to 30 days. The configuration for this snapshot includes options to ignore unavailable indices, exclude the global state, and create a partial snapshot. The maximum number of bytes that can be restored from this snapshot is set to 10 MB, and the maximum number of files that can be restored is set to 2000.

The "indices" section specifies which indices should be included in the snapshot. In this case, only indices starting with "my-index-" will be included. The "ignore_unavailable" option is set to true, which means that any unavailable indices will be ignored during the snapshot process. The "include_global_state" option is set to false, which means that the global state of the cluster (such as the number of nodes and their roles) will not be included in the snapshot. The "partial" option is set to false, which means that a complete snapshot of all indices will be created.

Overall, this YAML configuration file defines a snapshot repository and a snapshot that can be used for automated backups of an Elasticsearch cluster.
